LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CHARLES LEE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND, AMENDED COMPLAINT DUE WITHIN 10 DAYS. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED FACTS THAT SHE WAS NEGLECTED WITHIN THE MEANING OF THE STATUTE WHICH REQUIRES "ACTS OF EGREGIOUS ABUSE" ON A PAR WITH ACTS CITED AT E.G., CARTER V. LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CHARLES LEE'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT AS TO ITEM NOS. 1 - 7, 12, AND DENIED AS TO ITEM NOS. 8 - 11. HAVING RULED ON THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, THE MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS MOOT. THE SUBJECT PLEADINGS OF THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION ARE NOT IRRELEVANT, FALSE OR IMPROPER. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS UNOPPOSED AND IS ACCORDINGLY GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-01-2012 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT CHARLES LEE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS Sustained with leave to amend, amended complaint due within 10 days. Plaintiff has not alleged facts that she was neglected within the meaning of the statute which requires "acts of egregious abuse" on a par with acts cited at e.g., Carter v. Prime Healthcare, 198 Cal.App.4th 396, 405 et seq. (2011). ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Curtis Karnow, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976 =(302/CK)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-01-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT CHARLES LEE'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF COMPLAINT IS Off calendar as moot as to item nos. 1 - 7, 12, and denied as to item nos. 8 - 11. Having ruled on the demurrer to the first cause of action, the motion to strike portions of the first cause of action is moot. The subject pleadings of the second cause of action are not irrelevant, false or improper. Defendant's request for judicial notice is unopposed and is accordingly granted. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Curtis Karnow, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976 =(302/CK)
